name: Chili Bean Casserole
recipeIngredient: 2 cups frozen or canned corn, thawed and drained
1 4 oz can of sliced black olives, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
2. Cook rice according to package instructions.
3. Mix yogurt with cilantro and taco sauce in a bowl.
4. "Saute" onions and garlic in wine until soft.
5. In a casserole dish sprayed with PAM, layer: corn, cooked rice, olives, zucchini, onion/garlic mixture.
6. Pour beans over the layers.
7. Cover and bake in the oven for 30 minutes or until heated thoroughly.
8. Sprinkle tomatoes on top and drizzle with yogurt mixture.
9. Serve.
Makes: 4 main dish servings
Notes:
Buy fresh cilantro, wash, dry completely, chop, and freeze spread out on a piece of wax paper. Store in a zip lock baggie for easy access to fresh cilantro anytime.
I purchased the grocery store brand of "Mexican style chili beans," which have little to no fat.
Variations: Add shredded lettuce, scallions, or sautéed mushrooms with onions.
